preamble: The shift from the low front/central vowel [a] to the mid front vowel [e] is a process of Vowel Raising and Fronting. This is extremely common as part of i-mutation (i-umlaut) in Germanic languages, where a low vowel raised and fronted under the influence of a following high front sound.

examples.note: Old English plural *manniz underwent i-mutation of /a/ to /e/ (after an intermediate [æ] stage).
German primary Umlaut of /a/ to /e/ (written as 'ä' in modern German but phonetically often [ɛ] or [e]).
Fronting and raising of Proto-Turkic low vowel.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
